sutta: once while the exalted 1 dwelt among the barghes on crocodile hill and the exalted 1 with the deva eye surpassing mans in clearness saw him seated then nodding and as a strong man might bend his arm to and fro the exalted 1 vanished from the dear palm and appeared before the verbal maha mogulana and the exalted 1 sat down on the appointed seat and when he was seated he said to the verbal maha mauglana you were nodding mogallana you were dozing moghalana is 1 of very interesting and important suitas and this is a time when verbal maha had just become a disciple of the buddha not long and he had not yet become an arahant so he was practicing very hard and according to some other later books for 7 days after the the verbal maha mogalana became a disciple of the buddha he did not sleep he was practicing so hard now that he tried not to sleep at night and after the 7 days he attained so this doesn't mention how many days 10 hours after mahamola had joined the buddha and then the buddha said accordingly mawlana if while you abide thoughtful the thought comes drowsiness has descended take no heed of it make no addo of that thought and maybe as you abide so that drowsiness will pass if abiding soul it past not then you should ponder in your heart on dhamma as heard as mastered exploit with your mind review it and maybe as you abide so that drowsiness will pass if it pass not then you should repeat dhamma in detail as heard as mastered and maybe that drowsiness will pass if it pass not then you should pull both ear lobes and knead the limbs with the hand and maybe that drowsiness will pass if it pass not then you should arise from sitting cleanse the eyes with water survey the horizon and gaze up at the starry constellations and maybe that drowsiness will pass if it pass north then you should apply your mind to the thought of light fix your mind on the thought of day as by day so by night as by night so by day thus with mind unhindered unhampered you should make your thought become radiant and maybe that drowsiness will pass if it pass not then with the senses withdrawn the mind not outward gone you should fix your thought on the meditation walk conscious of it to and fro and maybe that drowsiness will pass if abiding so that drowsiness pass not then you should lie in light lie down on your right side foot covering foot mindful aware with mindset on the thought of arising and on awakening mogalana get up quickly thinking i'll not lift yoke to the pleasures of lying reclining and drowsiness verily let your training be dust mowglana moreover moglana let your training be thus not lifted up with pride will i visit families verily moghalana let your training beat us for in families mauglana there are many things to be done whereby the men may not notice amongst coming and if the monk visit lifted up with pride he'll think who now i wonder has embroiled me with this family these men have no have now no liking for me and so from getting nothing he becomes trouble being troubled he becomes worked up being worked up he becomes uncontrolled when uncontrolled the mind is far from concentration moreover mauglana let your training beat us i'll talk no provoking talk verily moglana let your training be thus when there is provoking thought mughlana much talk may be expected when there is much talk 1 becomes worked up being worked up 1 becomes uncontrolled when uncontrolled the mind is far from concentration mawlana i praise not fellowship just with all nor do i not pray such fellowship indeed mawlana i praise not fellowship with house dwellers with wanderers with dwellings that have but dwellings that have few sounds little noise are free from folks breath where 1 may live apart from man in befitting solitude it's such that i praise now when he had thus spoken the verbal maha magulana said this to the exalted 1 briefly lord how becomes a monk free by cravings destruction reaching the perpetual and the perpetual peace from effort the perpetual holy state the perpetual finality and becomes foremost among devas and men and the buddha said here in mughlana among has learned this it befits not to be engrossed in the things of this world if mawgalana among has learned this it befits not to be engrossed in the things of this world he recognizes each condition or each state the dhamma recognizing each condition he understands each condition understanding each condition whatever feeling he feels pleasant painful or neither he abides seeing the impermanence of those feelings viewing them dispassionately looking for their end regarding them as something to be renounced living in such contemplation he cleaves not to anything in the world not cleaving to anything he craves not not craving for anything he becomes just completely cool in himself and he knows birth is destroyed lift is the holy life done is what had to be done there is no more of this to come verily mawlana in this manner briefly among becomes freed by cravings destruction reaching the perpetual end the perpetual peace from effort the perpetual holy state the perpetual finality and becomes foremost among devas and men as the end of the sutta
commentary:
is quite an interesting and important suta here the buddha found the verb dosing so he gave some very practical advice he told the verbal maha mogul he said now if drown is drowsiness comes firstly take no heed of it just try to ignore that drowsiness but if you can't ignore the drowsiness then think about dharma review dharma that you have heard maybe the drowsiness will pass but if it doesn't then you should repeat dharma in detail you remember in the buddha's days there are there were no books so they had to repeat dhamma after they had learned they had to repeat it so here the buddha is saying the third thing to do is to repeat the dharma and then if by repeating the dharma the drowsiness still doesn't go away then the buddha said pull both ears pull both ears and and rub the limbs with the hands and massage the limbs with the hands and maybe to get the ear the blood flowing and maybe drowsiness will go away but if it does not then clean the eyes with water and look up into the sky and look at the stars etc and maybe the drowsiness will pass but if there is not again then thing of light that make the mind think of lightner so that um when the mind is bright now it does not it doesn't become sleepy but if that doesn't work now then the buddha said now walk take a walk take a meditation walk be mindful and i think a meditation work and if even by walking the drowsiness doesn't go away then finally the buddha said lie on the right side take a rest but get up quickly as soon as 1 wakes up then get up quickly don't lie on the bed most people tend to lie on the bus and enjoy the pleasure of uh lying down but the buddha said no don't enjoy so these are the practical steps the buddha gave then then the after that the buddha gave him another 3 types of advice the first 1 not to have pride when he visit families because the buddha said sometimes when a person when a monk goes to visit families on arms around sometimes they might not notice him and they have many things to do they might not notice him and i might not come out not to bring him food and he waits outside the door and doesn't get any food now if he has pride now he might think that these people are purposely not giving him food and then he becomes worked up so the buddha said not to have any pride the second 1 the buddha said not to talk any provoking talk we talk any provoking talk then there'll be a lot of talk and then from too much talk where 1 becomes unmindful and the mind doesn't become concentrated and thirdly the advice the buddha gave was to live a partner in seclusion but this generally not all monks can do that live a partner live in seclusion because you need a lot of discipline a monk doesn't have enough training and discipline then instead of practicing more he might tend to sleep more then on how to attain the final goal and the buddha said to remember that it is that 1 should be not engrossed should not be engrossed with the things of the world and then if 1 is not engrossed in the things of the world then 1 abides seeing the impermanence of feelings uh viewing them dispassionately and then on clips not to anything in the world and from that 1 does not crave for anything in the world and 1 let's go on renounces everything in the world and slowly 1 becomes completely cool
